1	"""Full Argus model assembly."""
     2	
     3	from __future__ import annotations
     4	
     5	from typing import Any
     6	
     7	import torch
     8	import torch.nn as nn
     9	
    10	from argus.model.board_detector import BoardDetector
    11	from argus.model.move_head import MoveHead
    12	from argus.model.oblique_square_decoder import ObliqueSquareQueryDecoder
    13	from argus.model.patch_pooling import PatchPoolingHead
    14	from argus.model.square_head import SquareHead
    15	from argus.model.temporal import MambaTemporalModule
    16	from argus.model.vision_encoder import VisionEncoder
    17	from argus.types import ModelOutput
    18	
    19	
    20	class ArgusModel(nn.Module):
    21	    """End-to-end model for multi-board chess move prediction from video."""
    22	
    23	    def __init__(
    24	        self,
    25	        vision_encoder_name: str = "facebook/dinov2-base",
    26	        vision_embed_dim: int | None = 768,
    27	        frozen_vision: bool = True,
    28	        temporal_d_model: int = 512,
    29	        temporal_n_layers: int = 6,
    30	        temporal_d_state: int = 128,
    31	        temporal_expand: int = 2,
    32	        move_vocab_size: int = 1970,
    33	        num_board_queries: int = 32,
    34	        detector_hidden_dim: int = 512,
    35	        detector_num_heads: int = 8,
    36	        detector_num_layers: int = 6,
    37	        identity_dim: int = 128,
    38	        pooling_type: str = "mean",
    39	        square_pool_size: int = 8,
    40	        square_head_enabled: bool = False,
    41	        square_vocab_size: int = 13,
    42	        square_head_type: str = "simple_mlp",
    43	        square_head_hidden_dim: int = 512,
    44	        square_head_transformer_layers: int = 2,
    45	        square_head_transformer_heads: int = 8,
    46	        square_head_transformer_ff_dim: int = 1024,
    47	        square_head_dropout: float = 0.1,
    48	        square_token_mode: str = "pooled",
    49	        square_query_num_heads: int = 8,
    50	        square_query_dropout: float = 0.0,
    51	        square_query_mlp_ratio: float = 4.0,
    52	        use_detector: bool = False,
    53	        vision_encoder_type: str = "dinov2",
    54	        vision_feature_layer_indices: list[int] | tuple[int, ...] | None = None,
    55	        vision_output_grid_size: int = 14,
    56	    ) -> None:
    57	        super().__init__()
    58	        self.use_detector = use_detector
    59	        self.square_token_mode = square_token_mode
    60	        self.vision_encoder = VisionEncoder(
    61	            model_name=vision_encoder_name,
    62	            frozen=frozen_vision,
    63	            embed_dim=vision_embed_dim,
    64	            encoder_type=vision_encoder_type,
    65	            feature_layer_indices=vision_feature_layer_indices,
    66	            output_grid_size=vision_output_grid_size,
    67	        )
    68	        resolved_embed_dim = self.vision_encoder.embed_dim
    69	        feature_layers = None
    70	        if vision_feature_layer_indices is not None:
    71	            feature_layers = [int(idx) for idx in vision_feature_layer_indices]
    72	        self._model_config = {
    73	            "vision_encoder_name": vision_encoder_name,
    74	            "vision_encoder_type": vision_encoder_type,
    75	            "vision_embed_dim": resolved_embed_dim,
    76	            "vision_feature_layer_indices": feature_layers,
    77	            "vision_output_grid_size": vision_output_grid_size,
    78	            "frozen_vision": frozen_vision,
    79	            "temporal_d_model": temporal_d_model,
    80	            "temporal_n_layers": temporal_n_layers,
    81	            "temporal_d_state": temporal_d_state,
    82	            "temporal_expand": temporal_expand,
    83	            "move_vocab_size": move_vocab_size,
    84	            "num_board_queries": num_board_queries,
    85	            "detector_hidden_dim": detector_hidden_dim,
    86	            "detector_num_heads": detector_num_heads,
    87	            "detector_num_layers": detector_num_layers,
    88	            "identity_dim": identity_dim,
    89	            "pooling_type": pooling_type,
    90	            "square_pool_size": square_pool_size,
    91	            "square_head_enabled": square_head_enabled,
    92	            "square_vocab_size": square_vocab_size,
    93	            "square_head_type": square_head_type,
    94	            "square_head_hidden_dim": square_head_hidden_dim,
    95	            "square_head_transformer_layers": square_head_transformer_layers,
    96	            "square_head_transformer_heads": square_head_transformer_heads,
    97	            "square_head_transformer_ff_dim": square_head_transformer_ff_dim,
    98	            "square_head_dropout": square_head_dropout,
    99	            "square_token_mode": square_token_mode,
   100	            "square_query_num_heads": square_query_num_heads,
   101	            "square_query_dropout": square_query_dropout,
   102	            "square_query_mlp_ratio": square_query_mlp_ratio,
   103	            "use_detector": use_detector,
   104	        }
   105	        self.patch_pooling = PatchPoolingHead(
   106	            embed_dim=resolved_embed_dim,
   107	            pooling_type=pooling_type,
   108	            square_size=square_pool_size,
   109	        )
   110	        self.square_tokenizer = self._build_square_tokenizer(
   111	            embed_dim=resolved_embed_dim,
   112	            square_token_mode=square_token_mode,
   113	            square_query_num_heads=square_query_num_heads,
   114	            square_query_dropout=square_query_dropout,
   115	            square_query_mlp_ratio=square_query_mlp_ratio,
   116	        )
   117	        self.temporal = MambaTemporalModule(
   118	            d_model=temporal_d_model,
   119	            n_layers=temporal_n_layers,
   120	            d_state=temporal_d_state,
   121	            expand=temporal_expand,
   122	            input_dim=resolved_embed_dim,
   123	        )
   124	        self.move_head = MoveHead(hidden_dim=temporal_d_model, vocab_size=move_vocab_size)
   125	        self.square_head = (
   126	            SquareHead(
   127	                embed_dim=resolved_embed_dim,
   128	                num_classes=square_vocab_size,
   129	                head_type=square_head_type,
   130	                hidden_dim=square_head_hidden_dim,
   131	                transformer_layers=square_head_transformer_layers,
   132	                transformer_heads=square_head_transformer_heads,
   133	                transformer_ff_dim=square_head_transformer_ff_dim,
   134	                dropout=square_head_dropout,
   135	            )
   136	            if square_head_enabled
   137	            else None
   138	        )
   139	        if use_detector:
   140	            self.board_detector = BoardDetector(
   141	                num_queries=num_board_queries,
   142	                hidden_dim=detector_hidden_dim,
   143	                num_heads=detector_num_heads,
   144	                num_decoder_layers=detector_num_layers,
   145	                identity_dim=identity_dim,
   146	                input_dim=resolved_embed_dim,
   147	            )
   148	
   149	    def _build_square_tokenizer(
   150	        self,
   151	        *,
   152	        embed_dim: int,
   153	        square_token_mode: str,
   154	        square_query_num_heads: int,
   155	        square_query_dropout: float,
   156	        square_query_mlp_ratio: float,
   157	    ) -> ObliqueSquareQueryDecoder | None:
   158	        if square_token_mode == "pooled":
   159	            return None
   160	        if square_token_mode == "oblique_square_queries":
   161	            return ObliqueSquareQueryDecoder(
   162	                embed_dim=embed_dim,
   163	                num_heads=square_query_num_heads,
   164	                dropout=square_query_dropout,
   165	                mlp_ratio=square_query_mlp_ratio,
   166	            )
   167	        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported square_token_mode: {square_token_mode}")
   168	
   169	    def _square_tokens_from_patches(
   170	        self,
   171	        patch_tokens: torch.Tensor,
   172	        *,
   173	        board_corners: torch.Tensor | None,
   174	        image_height: int,
   175	        image_width: int,
   176	    ) -> torch.Tensor | None:
   177	        if self.square_tokenizer is not None:
   178	            square_tokens: torch.Tensor = self.square_tokenizer(
   179	                patch_tokens,
   180	                corners=board_corners,
   181	                image_size=(image_height, image_width),
   182	            )
   183	            return square_tokens
   184	        if self.square_head is not None or self.patch_pooling.pooling_type == "square_attention":
   185	            square_tokens = self.patch_pooling.to_square_tokens(patch_tokens)
   186	            return square_tokens
   187	        return None
   188	
   189	    def forward_single_board(
   190	        self,
   191	        crops: torch.Tensor,
   192	        legal_masks: torch.Tensor | None = None,
   193	        board_corners: torch.Tensor | None = None,
   194	    ) -> ModelOutput:
   195	        batch_size, seq_len, _channels, image_height, image_width = crops.shape
   196	        flat_crops = crops.reshape(batch_size * seq_len, crops.shape[2], image_height, image_width)
   197	        patch_tokens = self.vision_encoder.forward_patches(flat_crops)
   198	        flat_board_corners = None
   199	        if board_corners is not None:
   200	            flat_board_corners = board_corners.reshape(batch_size * seq_len, 4, 2)
   201	
   202	        square_tokens = self._square_tokens_from_patches(
   203	            patch_tokens,
   204	            board_corners=flat_board_corners,
   205	            image_height=image_height,
   206	            image_width=image_width,
   207	        )
   208	        square_logits = None
   209	        if self.square_head is not None:
   210	            if square_tokens is None:
   211	                raise RuntimeError("Square head requires square tokens, but none were produced")
   212	            square_logits = self.square_head(square_tokens).reshape(
   213	                batch_size,
   214	                seq_len,
   215	                -1,
   216	                self._model_config["square_vocab_size"],
   217	            )
   218	
   219	        if square_tokens is not None and self.square_tokenizer is not None:
   220	            features = self.patch_pooling.pool_square_tokens(square_tokens)
   221	        else:
   222	            features = self.patch_pooling(patch_tokens)
   223	        features = features.reshape(batch_size, seq_len, -1)
   224	        temporal_features = self.temporal(features)
   225	        move_logits, move_probs, detect_logits = self.move_head(temporal_features, legal_masks)
   226	        return ModelOutput(
   227	            move_logits=move_logits.unsqueeze(2),
   228	            move_probs=move_probs.unsqueeze(2),
   229	            detect_logits=detect_logits.unsqueeze(2),
   230	            square_logits=square_logits,
   231	        )
   232	
   233	    def forward_multi_board(
   234	        self,
   235	        frames: torch.Tensor,
   236	        board_crops: torch.Tensor | None = None,
   237	        board_ids: torch.Tensor | None = None,
   238	        legal_masks: torch.Tensor | None = None,
   239	    ) -> ModelOutput:
   240	        batch_size, seq_len, channels, image_height, image_width = frames.shape
   241	        flat_frames = frames.reshape(batch_size * seq_len, channels, image_height, image_width)
   242	        patch_features = self.vision_encoder.forward_patches(flat_frames)
   243	        bboxes, confidences, identities = self.board_detector(patch_features)
   244	        num_queries = bboxes.shape[1]
   245	        bboxes = bboxes.reshape(batch_size, seq_len, num_queries, 4)
   246	        confidences = confidences.reshape(batch_size, seq_len, num_queries)
   247	        identities = identities.reshape(batch_size, seq_len, num_queries, -1)
   248	
   249	        if board_crops is not None:
   250	            num_boards = board_crops.shape[2]
   251	            flat_crops = board_crops.reshape(
   252	                batch_size * seq_len * num_boards, channels, image_height, image_width
   253	            )
   254	            patch_tokens = self.vision_encoder.forward_patches(flat_crops)
   255	            crop_features = self.patch_pooling(patch_tokens)
   256	            crop_features = crop_features.reshape(batch_size, seq_len, num_boards, -1)
   257	            all_move_logits, all_move_probs, all_detect_logits = [], [], []
   258	            for board_idx in range(num_boards):
   259	                board_features = crop_features[:, :, board_idx, :]
   260	                temporal_out = self.temporal(board_features)
   261	                board_masks = legal_masks[:, :, board_idx, :] if legal_masks is not None else None
   262	                m_logits, m_probs, d_logits = self.move_head(temporal_out, board_masks)
   263	                all_move_logits.append(m_logits)
   264	                all_move_probs.append(m_probs)
   265	                all_detect_logits.append(d_logits)
   266	            move_logits = torch.stack(all_move_logits, dim=2)
   267	            move_probs = torch.stack(all_move_probs, dim=2)
   268	            detect_logits = torch.stack(all_detect_logits, dim=2)
   269	        else:
   270	            raise NotImplementedError("ROI pooling from detected bboxes not yet implemented.")
   271	
   272	        return ModelOutput(
   273	            move_logits=move_logits,
   274	            move_probs=move_probs,
   275	            detect_logits=detect_logits,
   276	            board_bboxes=bboxes,
   277	            board_confidence=confidences,
   278	            board_identity=identities,
   279	        )
   280	
   281	    @property
   282	    def model_config(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   283	        return dict(self._model_config)
   284	
   285	    @classmethod
   286	    def from_config(cls, model_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> ArgusModel:
   287	        return cls(**(model_config or {}))
   288	
   289	    def forward(
   290	        self,
   291	        crops: torch.Tensor | None = None,
   292	        frames: torch.Tensor | None = None,
   293	        legal_masks: torch.Tensor | None = None,
   294	        board_crops: torch.Tensor | None = None,
   295	        board_ids: torch.Tensor | None = None,
   296	        board_corners: torch.Tensor | None = None,
   297	    ) -> ModelOutput:
   298	        if crops is not None:
   299	            return self.forward_single_board(crops, legal_masks, board_corners=board_corners)
   300	        if frames is not None and self.use_detector:
   301	            return self.forward_multi_board(frames, board_crops, board_ids, legal_masks)
   302	        raise ValueError("Provide 'crops' (single-board) or 'frames' with use_detector=True")

1	"""Chess-constrained move prediction head."""
     2	
     3	from __future__ import annotations
     4	
     5	import torch
     6	import torch.nn as nn
     7	import torch.nn.functional as F
     8	
     9	from argus.chess.constraint_mask import apply_constraint_mask
    10	from argus.chess.move_vocabulary import VOCAB_SIZE
    11	
    12	
    13	class MoveHead(nn.Module):
    14	    """Predicts chess moves with legality constraints."""
    15	
    16	    def __init__(self, hidden_dim: int = 512, vocab_size: int = VOCAB_SIZE) -> None:
    17	        super().__init__()
    18	        self.move_proj = nn.Sequential(
    19	            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim),
    20	            nn.GELU(),
    21	            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, vocab_size),
    22	        )
    23	        self.detect_proj = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, 1)
    24	
    25	    def forward(
    26	        self,
    27	        features: torch.Tensor,
    28	        legal_masks: torch.Tensor | None = None,
    29	    ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
    30	        move_logits = self.move_proj(features)
    31	        detect_logits = self.detect_proj(features).squeeze(-1)
    32	        if legal_masks is not None:
    33	            masked_logits = apply_constraint_mask(move_logits, legal_masks)
    34	            move_probs = F.softmax(masked_logits, dim=-1)
    35	        else:
    36	            move_probs = F.softmax(move_logits, dim=-1)
    37	        return move_logits, move_probs, detect_logits
    38	
    39	    def predict(
    40	        self,
    41	        features: torch.Tensor,
    42	        legal_masks: torch.Tensor,
    43	        threshold: float = 0.5,
    44	    ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
    45	        _, move_probs, detect_logits = self.forward(features, legal_masks)
    46	        predicted_indices = move_probs.argmax(dim=-1)
    47	        confidences = move_probs.gather(-1, predicted_indices.unsqueeze(-1)).squeeze(-1)
    48	        move_detected = torch.sigmoid(detect_logits) > threshold
    49	        return predicted_indices, confidences, move_detected

1	"""Temporal module using Mamba-2 State Space Model."""
     2	
     3	from __future__ import annotations
     4	
     5	import torch
     6	import torch.nn as nn
     7	
     8	try:
     9	    from mamba_ssm import Mamba2
    10	
    11	    MAMBA_AVAILABLE = True
    12	except ImportError:
    13	    MAMBA_AVAILABLE = False
    14	
    15	
    16	class MambaTemporalModule(nn.Module):
    17	    """Mamba-2 SSM for temporal state tracking."""
    18	
    19	    def __init__(
    20	        self,
    21	        d_model: int = 512,
    22	        n_layers: int = 6,
    23	        d_state: int = 128,
    24	        expand: int = 2,
    25	        input_dim: int | None = None,
    26	    ) -> None:
    27	        super().__init__()
    28	        self.d_model = d_model
    29	
    30	        self.input_proj: nn.Module
    31	        if input_dim is not None and input_dim != d_model:
    32	            self.input_proj = nn.Linear(input_dim, d_model)
    33	        else:
    34	            self.input_proj = nn.Identity()
    35	
    36	        self.layers = nn.ModuleList()
    37	        self.norms = nn.ModuleList()
    38	
    39	        if MAMBA_AVAILABLE:
    40	            for _ in range(n_layers):
    41	                self.layers.append(Mamba2(d_model=d_model, d_state=d_state, expand=expand))
    42	                self.norms.append(nn.LayerNorm(d_model))
    43	        else:
    44	            for _ in range(n_layers):
    45	                self.layers.append(
    46	                    nn.GRU(input_size=d_model, hidden_size=d_model, batch_first=True)
    47	                )
    48	                self.norms.append(nn.LayerNorm(d_model))
    49	
    50	        self.final_norm = nn.LayerNorm(d_model)
    51	        self._is_mamba = MAMBA_AVAILABLE
    52	
    53	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    54	        x = self.input_proj(x)
    55	        for norm, layer in zip(self.norms, self.layers):
    56	            residual = x
    57	            x = norm(x)
    58	            if self._is_mamba:
    59	                x = layer(x)
    60	            else:
    61	                x, _ = layer(x)
    62	            x = x + residual
    63	        result: torch.Tensor = self.final_norm(x)
    64	        return result

1	"""Vision encoders for per-frame board feature extraction."""
     2	
     3	from __future__ import annotations
     4	
     5	import inspect
     6	from collections.abc import Sequence
     7	from pathlib import Path
     8	from typing import Any, Protocol, cast

    10	import torch
    11	import torch.nn as nn
    12	import torch.nn.functional as F

    26	else:

    28	
    29	_WEIGHTS_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent / "weights"
    30	_IMAGENET_MEAN = (0.485, 0.456, 0.406)
    31	_IMAGENET_STD = (0.229, 0.224, 0.225)
    32	_SIGLIP_MEAN = (0.5, 0.5, 0.5)
    33	_SIGLIP_STD = (0.5, 0.5, 0.5)
    34	
    35	DEFAULT_DINOV2_MODEL = "facebook/dinov2-base"
    36	DEFAULT_DINOV3_MODEL = "facebook/dinov3-vitb16-pretrain-lvd1689m"
    37	DEFAULT_SIGLIP_MODEL = "google/siglip-base-patch16-224"
    38	DEFAULT_SIGLIP2_MODEL = "google/siglip2-base-patch16-naflex"
    39	DEFAULT_YOLO_MODEL = "weights/yolo_base/yolo11n.pt"
    40	
    41	
    42	def default_model_name_for_encoder_type(encoder_type: str) -> str:
    43	    normalized = encoder_type.lower()
    44	    if normalized in {"dino", "dinov2"}:
    45	        return DEFAULT_DINOV2_MODEL
    46	    if normalized == "dinov3":
    47	        return DEFAULT_DINOV3_MODEL
    48	    if normalized == "siglip":
    49	        return DEFAULT_SIGLIP_MODEL
    50	    if normalized == "siglip2":
    51	        return DEFAULT_SIGLIP2_MODEL
    52	    if normalized == "yolo":
    53	        return DEFAULT_YOLO_MODEL
    54	    raise ValueError(f"Unsupported encoder_type: {encoder_type}")
    55	
    56	
    57	def _has_transformers_weights(directory: Path) -> bool:
    58	    weight_filenames = (
    59	        "model.safetensors",
    60	        "pytorch_model.bin",
    61	        "model.safetensors.index.json",
    62	        "pytorch_model.bin.index.json",
    63	    )
    64	    return any((directory / filename).exists() for filename in weight_filenames)
    65	
    66	
    67	def _resolve_dino_model_path(model_name: str) -> str:
    68	    """Return a local DINO weights path if available, otherwise the HF repo id."""
    69	    short = model_name.split("/")[-1]
    70	    local = _WEIGHTS_ROOT / short
    71	    if (local / "config.json").exists() and _has_transformers_weights(local):
    72	        return str(local)
    73	    return model_name
    74	
    75	
    76	def _resolve_siglip_model_path(model_name: str) -> str:
    77	    """Return a local SigLIP weights path if available, otherwise the HF repo id."""
    78	    short = model_name.split("/")[-1]
    79	    local = _WEIGHTS_ROOT / short
    80	    if (local / "config.json").exists() and _has_transformers_weights(local):
    81	        return str(local)
    82	    cache_path = _resolve_hf_cache_model_path(model_name)
    83	    if cache_path is not None:
    84	        return cache_path
    85	    return model_name
    86	
    87	
    88	def _resolve_hf_cache_model_path(model_name: str) -> str | None:
    89	    if "/" not in model_name:
    90	        return None
    91	    hub_root = Path.home() / ".cache" / "huggingface" / "hub"
    92	    model_root = hub_root / f"models--{model_name.replace('/', '--')}"
    93	    if not model_root.exists():
    94	        return None
    95	    refs_main = model_root / "refs" / "main"
    96	    if refs_main.exists():
    97	        snapshot = model_root / "snapshots" / refs_main.read_text().strip()
    98	        if snapshot.exists() and _has_transformers_weights(snapshot):
    99	            return str(snapshot)
   100	    snapshots_dir = model_root / "snapshots"
   101	    if not snapshots_dir.exists():
   102	        return None
   103	    snapshots = sorted(
   104	        path
   105	        for path in snapshots_dir.iterdir()
   106	        if path.is_dir() and _has_transformers_weights(path)
   107	    )
   108	    if not snapshots:
   109	        return None
   110	    return str(snapshots[-1])
   111	
   112	
   113	def _resolve_yolo_model_path(model_name: str) -> str:
   114	    """Return a local YOLO checkpoint path if available."""
   115	    candidate = Path(model_name)
   116	    if candidate.exists():
   117	        return str(candidate)
   118	    local = _WEIGHTS_ROOT / model_name
   119	    if local.exists():
   120	        return str(local)
   121	    return model_name
   122	
   123	
   124	def _load_transformers_config(model_name: str) -> Any:
   125	    try:
   126	        return AutoConfig.from_pretrained(model_name, local_files_only=True)
   127	    except OSError:
   128	        return AutoConfig.from_pretrained(model_name)
   129	
   130	
   131	def _load_siglip_vision_model(model_name: str) -> nn.Module:
   132	    config = _load_transformers_config(model_name)
   133	    model_type = getattr(config, "model_type", None)
   134	    try:
   135	        if model_type == "siglip":
   136	            return cast(
   137	                nn.Module,
   138	                SiglipModel.from_pretrained(model_name, local_files_only=True).vision_model,
   139	            )
   140	        if model_type == "siglip_vision_model":
   141	            return SiglipVisionModel.from_pretrained(model_name, local_files_only=True)
   142	    except OSError:
   143	        if model_type == "siglip":
   144	            return cast(nn.Module, SiglipModel.from_pretrained(model_name).vision_model)
   145	        if model_type == "siglip_vision_model":
   146	            return SiglipVisionModel.from_pretrained(model_name)
   147	    raise ValueError(
   148	        f"Requested a SigLIP encoder for {model_name!r}, but config model_type is {model_type!r}"
   149	    )
   150	
   151	
   152	def _load_siglip2_vision_model(model_name: str) -> nn.Module:
   153	    config = _load_transformers_config(model_name)
   154	    model_type = getattr(config, "model_type", None)
   155	    if model_type not in {"siglip2", "siglip2_vision_model"}:
   156	        raise ValueError(
   157	            "Requested encoder_type='siglip2' for"
   158	            f" {model_name!r}, but config model_type is {model_type!r}."
   159	            " Pass a real SigLIP2 checkpoint such as"
   160	            f" {DEFAULT_SIGLIP2_MODEL!r}; do not use SigLIP-compatible FixRes"
   161	            " checkpoints here."
   162	        )
   163	    try:
   164	        if model_type == "siglip2":
   165	            return cast(
   166	                nn.Module,
   167	                Siglip2Model.from_pretrained(model_name, local_files_only=True).vision_model,
   168	            )
   169	        return Siglip2VisionModel.from_pretrained(model_name, local_files_only=True)
   170	    except OSError:
   171	        if model_type == "siglip2":
   172	            return cast(nn.Module, Siglip2Model.from_pretrained(model_name).vision_model)
   173	        return Siglip2VisionModel.from_pretrained(model_name)
   174	
   175	
   176	def _forward_siglip_patch_tokens(
   177	    vision_model: Any,
   178	    pixel_values: torch.Tensor,
   179	    *,
   180	    feature_layer_indices: Sequence[int],
   181	) -> torch.Tensor:
   182	    hidden_states = vision_model.embeddings(
   183	        pixel_values,
   184	        interpolate_pos_encoding=True,
   185	    )
   186	    if not feature_layer_indices:
   187	        for layer in vision_model.encoder.layers:
   188	            hidden_states = layer(hidden_states, None)
   189	        return cast(torch.Tensor, vision_model.post_layernorm(hidden_states))
   190	
   191	    selected_states: list[torch.Tensor] = []
   192	    requested_indices = {int(index) for index in feature_layer_indices}
   193	    for layer_index, layer in enumerate(vision_model.encoder.layers):
   194	        hidden_states = layer(hidden_states, None)
   195	        if layer_index in requested_indices:
   196	            selected_states.append(vision_model.post_layernorm(hidden_states))
   197	    if not selected_states:
   198	        raise RuntimeError("SigLIP encoder did not produce the requested hidden states")
   199	    return torch.stack(selected_states, dim=0).mean(dim=0)
   200	
   201	
   202	class Dinov2Backbone(nn.Module):
   203	    """DINOv2 ViT wrapper exposing patch tokens."""
   204	
   205	    def __init__(
   206	        self,
   207	        model_name: str = DEFAULT_DINOV2_MODEL,
   208	        frozen: bool = True,
   209	        embed_dim: int | None = None,
   210	        feature_layer_indices: Sequence[int] | None = None,
   211	    ) -> None:
   212	        super().__init__()
   213	        resolved = _resolve_dino_model_path(model_name)
   214	        try:
   215	            self.model = Dinov2Model.from_pretrained(resolved, local_files_only=True)
   216	        except OSError:
   217	            self.model = Dinov2Model.from_pretrained(model_name)
   218	
   219	        hidden_size = int(self.model.config.hidden_size)
   220	        raw_feature_layers = feature_layer_indices or ()
   221	        self.feature_layer_indices = tuple(int(idx) for idx in raw_feature_layers)
   222	        num_hidden_layers = int(self.model.config.num_hidden_layers)
   223	        for layer_index in self.feature_layer_indices:
   224	            if layer_index < 0 or layer_index >= num_hidden_layers:
   225	                raise ValueError(
   226	                    "Invalid DINO feature layer index"
   227	                    f" {layer_index}; expected 0 <= idx < {num_hidden_layers}"
   228	                )
   229	        if embed_dim is not None and embed_dim != hidden_size:
   230	            raise ValueError(
   231	                f"Requested DINO embed_dim={embed_dim}, but model outputs {hidden_size}"
   232	            )
   233	        self.embed_dim = hidden_size
   234	        if frozen:
   235	            self.freeze()
   236	
   237	    def freeze(self) -> None:
   238	        for param in self.model.parameters():
   239	            param.requires_grad = False
   240	
   241	    def unfreeze_last_n_layers(self, n: int) -> None:
   242	        self.freeze()
   243	        if n <= 0:
   244	            return
   245	        for layer in self.model.encoder.layer[-n:]:
   246	            for param in layer.parameters():
   247	                param.requires_grad = True
   248	
   249	    def unfreeze(self) -> None:
   250	        for param in self.model.parameters():
   251	            param.requires_grad = True
   252	
   253	    def forward_patches(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   254	        if not self.feature_layer_indices:
   255	            outputs = self.model(pixel_values=x)
   256	            result: torch.Tensor = outputs.last_hidden_state
   257	            return result
   258	
   259	        outputs = self.model(pixel_values=x, output_hidden_states=True)
   260	        hidden_states = outputs.hidden_states
   261	        if hidden_states is None:
   262	            raise RuntimeError("DINO encoder did not return hidden states")
   263	        selected_states = [
   264	            hidden_states[layer_index + 1] for layer_index in self.feature_layer_indices
   265	        ]
   266	        result = torch.stack(selected_states, dim=0).mean(dim=0)
   267	        return result
   268	
   269	    def forward_pooled(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   270	        patches = self.forward_patches(x)
   271	        return patches[:, 1:, :].mean(dim=1)
   272	
   273	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, return_patches: bool = False) -> torch.Tensor:
   274	        if return_patches:
   275	            return self.forward_patches(x)
   276	        return self.forward_pooled(x)
   277	
   278	
   279	class Dinov3Backbone(nn.Module):
   280	    """DINOv3 ViT wrapper exposing patch tokens.
   281	
   282	    Mirrors `Dinov2Backbone`. DINOv3's default config has `num_register_tokens=0`
   283	    so the output shape matches DINOv2: [B, 1+num_patches, hidden_size]. If a
   284	    non-zero register-token count appears in the config, `forward_pooled` will
   285	    still pool over everything except position 0 (the CLS) which includes
   286	    registers — acceptable for classifier heads but worth revisiting if a
   287	    downstream task needs patch-only pooling.
   288	
   289	    Note: Meta's DINOv3 checkpoints on HuggingFace are gated. Run
   290	    ``huggingface-cli login`` with a token that has accepted the gate before
   291	    instantiating this backbone.
   292	    """
   293	
   294	    def __init__(
   295	        self,
   296	        model_name: str = DEFAULT_DINOV3_MODEL,
   297	        frozen: bool = True,
   298	        embed_dim: int | None = None,
   299	        feature_layer_indices: Sequence[int] | None = None,
   300	    ) -> None:
   301	        super().__init__()
   302	        if DINOv3ViTModel is None:
   303	            raise ImportError(
   304	                "DINOv3 requires transformers >= 5.0 (DINOv3ViTModel not importable)."
   305	            )
   306	        resolved = _resolve_dino_model_path(model_name)
   307	        # Note: `local_files_only=True` triggers a spurious size-mismatch error
   308	        # in transformers 5.x when combined with certain DINOv3 checkpoints.
   309	        # Let transformers use its default cache lookup instead.
   310	        self.model = DINOv3ViTModel.from_pretrained(resolved)
   311	
   312	        hidden_size = int(self.model.config.hidden_size)
   313	        raw_feature_layers = feature_layer_indices or ()
   314	        self.feature_layer_indices = tuple(int(idx) for idx in raw_feature_layers)
   315	        num_hidden_layers = int(self.model.config.num_hidden_layers)
   316	        for layer_index in self.feature_layer_indices:
   317	            if layer_index < 0 or layer_index >= num_hidden_layers:
   318	                raise ValueError(
   319	                    "Invalid DINOv3 feature layer index"
   320	                    f" {layer_index}; expected 0 <= idx < {num_hidden_layers}"
   321	                )
   322	        if embed_dim is not None and embed_dim != hidden_size:
   323	            raise ValueError(
   324	                f"Requested DINOv3 embed_dim={embed_dim}, but model outputs {hidden_size}"
   325	            )
   326	        self.embed_dim = hidden_size
   327	        self.num_register_tokens = int(getattr(self.model.config, "num_register_tokens", 0))
   328	        if frozen:
   329	            self.freeze()
   330	
   331	    def freeze(self) -> None:
   332	        for param in self.model.parameters():
   333	            param.requires_grad = False
   334	
   335	    def unfreeze_last_n_layers(self, n: int) -> None:
   336	        self.freeze()
   337	        if n <= 0:
   338	            return
   339	        # DINOv3ViTModel in transformers has `.layer` (not `.encoder.layer`).
   340	        for layer in self.model.layer[-n:]:
   341	            for param in layer.parameters():
   342	                param.requires_grad = True
   343	
   344	    def unfreeze(self) -> None:
   345	        for param in self.model.parameters():
   346	            param.requires_grad = True
   347	
   348	    def forward_patches(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   349	        if not self.feature_layer_indices:
   350	            outputs = self.model(pixel_values=x)
   351	            result: torch.Tensor = outputs.last_hidden_state
   352	            return result
   353	
   354	        outputs = self.model(pixel_values=x, output_hidden_states=True)
   355	        hidden_states = outputs.hidden_states
   356	        if hidden_states is None:
   357	            raise RuntimeError("DINOv3 encoder did not return hidden states")
   358	        selected_states = [
   359	            hidden_states[layer_index + 1] for layer_index in self.feature_layer_indices
   360	        ]
   361	        result = torch.stack(selected_states, dim=0).mean(dim=0)
   362	        return result
   363	
   364	    def forward_pooled(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   365	        patches = self.forward_patches(x)
   366	        # Skip CLS token at index 0. Register tokens (if any) follow CLS and
   367	        # precede the patch tokens. Pooling over 1: includes them; for a
   368	        # classifier head this is fine (registers carry global context).
   369	        skip = 1 + self.num_register_tokens
   370	        return patches[:, skip:, :].mean(dim=1)
   371	
   372	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, return_patches: bool = False) -> torch.Tensor:
   373	        if return_patches:
   374	            return self.forward_patches(x)
   375	        return self.forward_pooled(x)
   376	
   377	
   378	class _SiglipNormalizationMixin:
   379	    input_mean: torch.Tensor

   395	            persistent=False,

   410	            persistent=False,

   413	    def _prepare_inputs(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   414	        imagenet_mean = self.input_mean.to(device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype)
   415	        imagenet_std = self.input_std.to(device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype)
   416	        siglip_mean = self.siglip_mean.to(device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype)
   417	        siglip_std = self.siglip_std.to(device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype)
   418	        rgb = (x * imagenet_std + imagenet_mean).clamp(0.0, 1.0)
   419	        return (rgb - siglip_mean) / siglip_std

   536	
   537	    def unfreeze_last_n_layers(self, n: int) -> None:

   539	        if n <= 0:

   545	    def unfreeze(self) -> None:

   548	
   549	    def _resize_to_patch_grid(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   550	        _, _, height, width = x.shape
   551	        target_height = max(self.patch_size, round(height / self.patch_size) * self.patch_size)
   552	        target_width = max(self.patch_size, round(width / self.patch_size) * self.patch_size)
   553	        if target_height == height and target_width == width:
   554	            return x
   555	        return F.interpolate(
   556	            x,
   557	            size=(target_height, target_width),
   558	            mode="bilinear",
   559	            align_corners=False,
   560	        )
   561	
   562	    def _patchify(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
   563	        resized = self._resize_to_patch_grid(x)
   564	        batch_size, channels, height, width = resized.shape
   565	        patches_h = height // self.patch_size
   566	        patches_w = width // self.patch_size
   567	        patches = resized.unfold(2, self.patch_size, self.patch_size).unfold(
   568	            3,
   569	            self.patch_size,
   570	            self.patch_size,
   571	        )
   572	        patches = patches.permute(0, 2, 3, 1, 4, 5).reshape(
   573	            batch_size,
   574	            patches_h * patches_w,
   575	            channels * self.patch_size * self.patch_size,
   576	        )
   577	        attention_mask = torch.ones(
   578	            batch_size,
   579	            patches_h * patches_w,
   580	            dtype=torch.bool,
   581	            device=resized.device,
   582	        )
   583	        spatial_shapes = torch.tensor(
   584	            [[patches_h, patches_w]] * batch_size,
   585	            dtype=torch.long,
   586	            device=resized.device,
   587	        )
   588	        return patches, attention_mask, spatial_shapes
   589	
   590	    def forward_patches(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   591	        prepared = self._prepare_inputs(x)
   592	        patch_values, attention_mask, spatial_shapes = self._patchify(prepared)
   593	        if not self.feature_layer_indices:
   594	            outputs = self._forward_siglip2_model(
   595	                patch_values=patch_values,
   596	                attention_mask=attention_mask,
   597	                spatial_shapes=spatial_shapes,
   598	                output_hidden_states=False,
   599	            )
   600	            result: torch.Tensor = outputs.last_hidden_state
   601	            return result
   602	
   603	        outputs = self._forward_siglip2_model(
   604	            patch_values=patch_values,
   605	            attention_mask=attention_mask,
   606	            spatial_shapes=spatial_shapes,
   607	            output_hidden_states=True,
   608	        )
   609	        hidden_states = outputs.hidden_states
   610	        if hidden_states is None:
   611	            raise RuntimeError("SigLIP2 encoder did not return hidden states")

   613	            hidden_states[layer_index + 1] for layer_index in self.feature_layer_indices
   614	        ]
   615	        result = torch.stack(selected_states, dim=0).mean(dim=0)
   616	        return result
   617	
   618	    def _forward_siglip2_model(
   619	        self,
   620	        *,
   621	        patch_values: torch.Tensor,
   622	        attention_mask: torch.Tensor,
   623	        spatial_shapes: torch.Tensor,
   624	        output_hidden_states: bool,
   625	    ) -> Any:

   628	            "pixel_values": patch_values,
   629	            "spatial_shapes": spatial_shapes,
   630	            "output_hidden_states": output_hidden_states,
   631	        }
   632	        if "pixel_attention_mask" in signature.parameters:
   633	            kwargs["pixel_attention_mask"] = attention_mask
   634	        else:
   635	            kwargs["attention_mask"] = attention_mask
   636	        return self.model(**kwargs)
   637	
   638	    def forward_pooled(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   639	        patches = self.forward_patches(x)
   640	        return patches.mean(dim=1)
   641	
   642	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, return_patches: bool = False) -> torch.Tensor:
   643	        if return_patches:
   644	            return self.forward_patches(x)
   645	        return self.forward_pooled(x)

   648	class YoloBackbone(nn.Module):
   649	    """YOLO-derived multi-scale visual frontend.
   650	
   651	    The existing Argus data path normalizes inputs for DINO. To keep that path
   652	    fixed for the frontend comparison, this backbone first undoes ImageNet
   653	    normalization, then extracts multi-scale YOLO feature maps and fuses them
   654	    into a square token grid.
   655	    """
   656	
   657	    def __init__(
   658	        self,
   659	        model_name: str = DEFAULT_YOLO_MODEL,
   660	        frozen: bool = True,
   661	        feature_layer_indices: Sequence[int] | None = None,
   662	        output_grid_size: int = 14,
   663	    ) -> None:
   664	        super().__init__()
   665	        resolved = _resolve_yolo_model_path(model_name)
   666	        from ultralytics import YOLO  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
   667	
   668	        self.model: Any = YOLO(resolved).model
   669	        raw_feature_layers = feature_layer_indices or (16, 19, 22)
   670	        self.feature_layer_indices = tuple(int(idx) for idx in raw_feature_layers)
   671	        if len(self.feature_layer_indices) == 0:
   672	            raise ValueError("feature_layer_indices must not be empty")
   673	        self.output_grid_size = output_grid_size
   674	        self._stop_layer = max(self.feature_layer_indices)
   675	        self.register_buffer(
   676	            "input_mean",
   677	            torch.tensor(_IMAGENET_MEAN, dtype=torch.float32).view(1, 3, 1, 1),
   678	            persistent=False,
   679	        )
   680	        self.register_buffer(
   681	            "input_std",
   682	            torch.tensor(_IMAGENET_STD, dtype=torch.float32).view(1, 3, 1, 1),
   683	            persistent=False,
   684	        )
   685	        self.embed_dim = self._infer_embed_dim()
   686	        if frozen:
   687	            self.freeze()
   688	
   689	    def freeze(self) -> None:
   690	        for param in self.model.parameters():
   691	            param.requires_grad = False
   692	
   693	    def unfreeze_last_n_layers(self, n: int) -> None:
   694	        self.freeze()
   695	        if n <= 0:
   696	            return
   697	        layers = list(self.model.model[:-1])
   698	        for layer in layers[-n:]:
   699	            for param in layer.parameters():
   700	                param.requires_grad = True
   701	
   702	    def unfreeze(self) -> None:
   703	        for param in self.model.parameters():
   704	            param.requires_grad = True
   705	
   706	    def _infer_embed_dim(self) -> int:
   707	        training = self.model.training
   708	        self.model.eval()
   709	        with torch.no_grad():
   710	            feature_maps = self._forward_feature_maps(torch.zeros(1, 3, 224, 224))
   711	        if training:
   712	            self.model.train()
   713	        return int(sum(feature_map.shape[1] for feature_map in feature_maps))
   714	
   715	    def _prepare_inputs(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   716	        mean = cast(torch.Tensor, self.input_mean).to(device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype)
   717	        std = cast(torch.Tensor, self.input_std).to(device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype)
   718	        result = x * std + mean
   719	        return result.clamp(0.0, 1.0)
   720	
   721	    def _forward_feature_maps(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> list[torch.Tensor]:
   722	        x = self._prepare_inputs(x)
   723	        cached: list[torch.Tensor | None] = []
   724	        outputs: dict[int, torch.Tensor] = {}
   725	        for module in self.model.model:
   726	            if module.f != -1:
   727	                module_input = (
   728	                    cached[module.f]
   729	                    if isinstance(module.f, int)
   730	                    else [x if idx == -1 else cached[idx] for idx in module.f]
   731	                )
   732	            else:
   733	                module_input = x
   734	            x = module(module_input)
   735	            cached.append(x if module.i in self.model.save else None)
   736	            if module.i in self.feature_layer_indices:
   737	                outputs[module.i] = x
   738	            if module.i >= self._stop_layer:
   739	                break
   740	        return [outputs[idx] for idx in self.feature_layer_indices]
   741	
   742	    def _resize_feature_map(self, feature_map: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   743	        _, _, height, width = feature_map.shape
   744	        target = self.output_grid_size
   745	        if height == target and width == target:
   746	            return feature_map
   747	        return F.interpolate(
   748	            feature_map,
   749	            size=(target, target),
   750	            mode="bilinear",
   751	            align_corners=False,
   752	        )
   753	
   754	    def forward_patches(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   755	        feature_maps = self._forward_feature_maps(x)
   756	        resized = [self._resize_feature_map(feature_map) for feature_map in feature_maps]
   757	        fused = torch.cat(resized, dim=1)
   758	        tokens = fused.flatten(2).transpose(1, 2)
   759	        result: torch.Tensor = F.layer_norm(tokens, (tokens.shape[-1],))
   760	        return result
   761	
   762	    def forward_pooled(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   763	        patches = self.forward_patches(x)
   764	        return patches.mean(dim=1)
   765	
   766	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, return_patches: bool = False) -> torch.Tensor:
   767	        if return_patches:
   768	            return self.forward_patches(x)
   769	        return self.forward_pooled(x)
   770	
   771	
   772	class VisionBackboneProtocol(Protocol):
   773	    embed_dim: int
   774	
   775	    def freeze(self) -> None: ...
   776	    def unfreeze_last_n_layers(self, n: int) -> None: ...
   777	    def unfreeze(self) -> None: ...
   778	    def forward_patches(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor: ...
   779	    def forward_pooled(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor: ...
   780	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, return_patches: bool = False) -> torch.Tensor: ...
   781

   783	class VisionEncoder(nn.Module):
   784	    """Unified frontend wrapper for DINOv2, SigLIP, SigLIP2, and YOLO features."""
   785	
   786	    def __init__(
   787	        self,
   788	        model_name: str | None = None,
   789	        frozen: bool = True,
   790	        embed_dim: int | None = None,
   791	        encoder_type: str = "dinov2",
   792	        feature_layer_indices: Sequence[int] | None = None,
   793	        output_grid_size: int = 14,
   794	    ) -> None:
   795	        super().__init__()
   796	        normalized_type = encoder_type.lower()
   797	        self.encoder_type = normalized_type
   798	        if model_name is None:
   799	            model_name = default_model_name_for_encoder_type(normalized_type)
   800	
   801	        self.backend: VisionBackboneProtocol
   802	        if normalized_type in {"dino", "dinov2"}:
   803	            self.backend = Dinov2Backbone(
   804	                model_name=model_name,
   805	                frozen=frozen,
   806	                embed_dim=embed_dim,
   807	                feature_layer_indices=feature_layer_indices,
   808	            )
   809	        elif normalized_type == "dinov3":
   810	            self.backend = Dinov3Backbone(
   811	                model_name=model_name,
   812	                frozen=frozen,
   813	                embed_dim=embed_dim,
   814	                feature_layer_indices=feature_layer_indices,
   815	            )
   816	        elif normalized_type == "siglip":
   817	            self.backend = SiglipBackbone(
   818	                model_name=model_name,
   819	                frozen=frozen,
   820	                embed_dim=embed_dim,
   821	                feature_layer_indices=feature_layer_indices,
   822	            )
   823	        elif normalized_type == "siglip2":
   824	            self.backend = Siglip2Backbone(
   825	                model_name=model_name,
   826	                frozen=frozen,
   827	                embed_dim=embed_dim,
   828	                feature_layer_indices=feature_layer_indices,
   829	            )
   830	        elif normalized_type == "yolo":
   831	            self.backend = YoloBackbone(
   832	                model_name=model_name,
   833	                frozen=frozen,
   834	                feature_layer_indices=feature_layer_indices,
   835	                output_grid_size=output_grid_size,
   836	            )
   837	        else:
   838	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported encoder_type: {encoder_type}")
   839	
   840	        self.embed_dim = self.backend.embed_dim
   841	
   842	    def freeze(self) -> None:
   843	        self.backend.freeze()
   844	
   845	    def unfreeze_last_n_layers(self, n: int) -> None:
   846	        self.backend.unfreeze_last_n_layers(n)
   847	
   848	    def unfreeze(self) -> None:
   849	        self.backend.unfreeze()
   850	
   851	    def forward_patches(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   852	        return self.backend.forward_patches(x)
   853	
   854	    def forward_pooled(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   855	        return self.backend.forward_pooled(x)
   856	
   857	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, return_patches: bool = False) -> torch.Tensor:
   858	        return self.backend.forward(x, return_patches=return_patches)

1	"""Fixed enumeration of all possible UCI chess moves.
     2	
     3	The vocabulary maps each possible move to a unique index (0..1969).
     4	This mapping is deterministic and never changes — every component
     5	(model output head, loss function, metrics) depends on consistency.
     6	
     7	Vocabulary layout:
     8	  - Indices 0..1967: all legal UCI moves (source_square + target_square + optional promotion)
     9	  - Index 1968: NO_MOVE (most frames have no move)
    10	  - Index 1969: UNKNOWN (occlusion-uncertain frames)
    11	"""
    12	
    13	from __future__ import annotations
    14	
    15	import chess
    16	
    17	NO_MOVE_IDX = 1968
    18	UNKNOWN_IDX = 1969
    19	VOCAB_SIZE = 1970
    20	
    21	
    22	class MoveVocabulary:
    23	    """Bijective mapping between UCI move strings and vocabulary indices."""
    24	
    25	    def __init__(self) -> None:
    26	        self._uci_to_idx: dict[str, int] = {}
    27	        self._idx_to_uci: dict[int, str] = {}
    28	        self._build()
    29	
    30	    def _build(self) -> None:
    31	        """Enumerate all possible UCI moves deterministically.
    32	
    33	        Only moves reachable by some chess piece are included:
    34	        - Same rank or file (rook/queen)
    35	        - Same diagonal (bishop/queen)
    36	        - Knight L-shape (rank_diff, file_diff) in {(1,2), (2,1)}
    37	        - King moves are a subset of queen moves (distance 1)
    38	        - Pawn moves are subsets of the above, plus promotion suffixes
    39	
    40	        This produces exactly 1968 unique moves for standard chess.
    41	        """
    42	        moves: list[str] = []
    43	
    44	        for src in chess.SQUARES:
    45	            for dst in chess.SQUARES:
    46	                if src == dst:
    47	                    continue
    48	
    49	                src_name = chess.square_name(src)
    50	                dst_name = chess.square_name(dst)
    51	                src_rank = chess.square_rank(src)
    52	                dst_rank = chess.square_rank(dst)
    53	                src_file = chess.square_file(src)
    54	                dst_file = chess.square_file(dst)
    55	
    56	                rank_diff = abs(src_rank - dst_rank)
    57	                file_diff = abs(src_file - dst_file)
    58	
    59	                # Only include moves reachable by some chess piece
    60	                is_reachable = (
    61	                    src_rank == dst_rank  # rook/queen horizontal
    62	                    or src_file == dst_file  # rook/queen vertical
    63	                    or rank_diff == file_diff  # bishop/queen diagonal
    64	                    or (rank_diff, file_diff) in ((1, 2), (2, 1))  # knight
    65	                )
    66	                if not is_reachable:
    67	                    continue
    68	
    69	                # Check if this could be a pawn promotion move
    70	                is_promotion = (dst_rank == 7 and src_rank == 6 and file_diff <= 1) or (
    71	                    dst_rank == 0 and src_rank == 1 and file_diff <= 1
    72	                )
    73	
    74	                # Base move is always included (queen/rook/bishop can land here)
    75	                moves.append(f"{src_name}{dst_name}")
    76	
    77	                # Promotion variants are additional entries
    78	                if is_promotion:
    79	                    for promo in ["q", "r", "b", "n"]:
    80	                        moves.append(f"{src_name}{dst_name}{promo}")
    81	
    82	        # Sort for determinism
    83	        moves.sort()
    84	
    85	        for idx, uci in enumerate(moves):
    86	            self._uci_to_idx[uci] = idx
    87	            self._idx_to_uci[idx] = uci
    88	
    89	        # Special tokens
    90	        self._uci_to_idx["<no_move>"] = NO_MOVE_IDX
    91	        self._idx_to_uci[NO_MOVE_IDX] = "<no_move>"
    92	        self._uci_to_idx["<unknown>"] = UNKNOWN_IDX
    93	        self._idx_to_uci[UNKNOWN_IDX] = "<unknown>"
    94	
    95	        assert len(self._idx_to_uci) == VOCAB_SIZE, (
    96	            f"Expected {VOCAB_SIZE} entries, got {len(self._idx_to_uci)}"
    97	        )
    98	
    99	    def uci_to_index(self, uci: str) -> int:
   100	        """Convert a UCI move string to its vocabulary index."""
   101	        return self._uci_to_idx[uci]
   102	
   103	    def index_to_uci(self, idx: int) -> str:
   104	        """Convert a vocabulary index to its UCI move string."""
   105	        return self._idx_to_uci[idx]
   106	
   107	    def contains(self, uci: str) -> bool:
   108	        """Check if a UCI string is in the vocabulary."""
   109	        return uci in self._uci_to_idx
   110	
   111	    @property
   112	    def size(self) -> int:
   113	        return VOCAB_SIZE
   114	
   115	    @property
   116	    def num_moves(self) -> int:
   117	        """Number of actual chess moves (excluding special tokens)."""
   118	        return NO_MOVE_IDX
   119	
   120	
   121	# Module-level singleton for convenience
   122	_VOCAB: MoveVocabulary | None = None
   123	
   124	
   125	def get_vocabulary() -> MoveVocabulary:
   126	    """Get the singleton move vocabulary instance."""
   127	    global _VOCAB
   128	    if _VOCAB is None:
   129	        _VOCAB = MoveVocabulary()
   130	    return _VOCAB

1	"""Generate legal move masks from chess board state.
     2	
     3	The constraint mask is a binary vector over the move vocabulary that
     4	zeros out illegal moves. This is applied to model logits before softmax,
     5	making it architecturally impossible to predict an illegal move.
     6	"""
     7	
     8	from __future__ import annotations
     9	
    10	import chess
    11	import torch
    12	
    13	from argus.chess.move_vocabulary import NO_MOVE_IDX, VOCAB_SIZE, get_vocabulary
    14	
    15	
    16	def get_legal_mask(board: chess.Board) -> torch.Tensor:
    17	    """Generate a binary mask over the move vocabulary for legal moves.
    18	
    19	    Args:
    20	        board: Current chess board position.
    21	
    22	    Returns:
    23	        Boolean tensor of shape (VOCAB_SIZE,) where True = legal.
    24	        NO_MOVE is always legal. UNKNOWN is always illegal.
    25	    """
    26	    vocab = get_vocabulary()
    27	    mask = torch.zeros(VOCAB_SIZE, dtype=torch.bool)
    28	
    29	    # NO_MOVE is always legal (most frames have no move)
    30	    mask[NO_MOVE_IDX] = True
    31	
    32	    for move in board.legal_moves:
    33	        uci = move.uci()
    34	        if vocab.contains(uci):
    35	            mask[vocab.uci_to_index(uci)] = True
    36	
    37	    return mask
    38	
    39	
    40	def get_legal_mask_batch(boards: list[chess.Board]) -> torch.Tensor:
    41	    """Generate legal move masks for a batch of boards.
    42	
    43	    Args:
    44	        boards: List of chess board positions.
    45	
    46	    Returns:
    47	        Boolean tensor of shape (len(boards), VOCAB_SIZE).
    48	    """
    49	    return torch.stack([get_legal_mask(b) for b in boards])
    50	
    51	
    52	def apply_constraint_mask(
    53	    logits: torch.Tensor,
    54	    mask: torch.Tensor,
    55	    fill_value: float = -1e9,
    56	) -> torch.Tensor:
    57	    """Apply legal move mask to logits, setting illegal moves to fill_value.
    58	
    59	    Args:
    60	        logits: Raw model output, shape (..., VOCAB_SIZE).
    61	        mask: Boolean mask, shape (..., VOCAB_SIZE). True = legal.
    62	        fill_value: Value to assign to illegal moves (large negative for softmax).
    63	
    64	    Returns:
    65	        Masked logits with same shape as input.
    66	    """
    67	    return logits.masked_fill(~mask, fill_value)

messages.content:
1	"""Per-game chess state tracker wrapping python-chess.
     2	
     3	Each tracked game gets one GameStateMachine instance. It is the
     4	authoritative source of legality and state — the model proposes
     5	moves, the state machine validates and commits them.
     6	"""
     7	
     8	from __future__ import annotations
     9	
    10	import chess
    11	import chess.pgn
    12	import torch
    13	
    14	from argus.chess.constraint_mask import get_legal_mask
    15	from argus.chess.move_vocabulary import VOCAB_SIZE, get_vocabulary
    16	
    17	
    18	class GameStateMachine:
    19	    """Tracks a single chess game's state and enforces legality."""
    20	
    21	    def __init__(self, fen: str | None = None) -> None:
    22	        """Initialize with optional starting FEN (defaults to standard start)."""
    23	        if fen is not None:
    24	            self.board = chess.Board(fen)
    25	        else:
    26	            self.board = chess.Board()
    27	        self._move_history: list[chess.Move] = []
    28	        self._initial_fen = self.board.fen()
    29	
    30	    def get_legal_mask(self) -> torch.Tensor:
    31	        """Get the legal move mask for the current position.
    32	
    33	        Returns:
    34	            Boolean tensor of shape (VOCAB_SIZE,).
    35	        """
    36	        return get_legal_mask(self.board)
    37	
    38	    def is_legal(self, uci: str) -> bool:
    39	        """Check if a UCI move is legal in the current position."""
    40	        try:
    41	            move = chess.Move.from_uci(uci)
    42	            return move in self.board.legal_moves
    43	        except (ValueError, chess.InvalidMoveError):
    44	            return False
    45	
    46	    def push_move(self, uci: str) -> bool:
    47	        """Attempt to push a move. Returns True if legal and applied.
    48	
    49	        Args:
    50	            uci: UCI move string (e.g. "e2e4").
    51	
    52	        Returns:
    53	            True if move was legal and applied, False otherwise.
    54	        """
    55	        try:
    56	            move = chess.Move.from_uci(uci)
    57	            if move not in self.board.legal_moves:
    58	                return False
    59	            self.board.push(move)
    60	            self._move_history.append(move)
    61	            return True
    62	        except (ValueError, chess.InvalidMoveError):
    63	            return False
    64	
    65	    def get_fen(self) -> str:
    66	        """Get the current FEN string."""
    67	        return self.board.fen()
    68	
    69	    def get_legal_moves_uci(self) -> list[str]:
    70	        """Get all legal moves as UCI strings."""
    71	        return [m.uci() for m in self.board.legal_moves]
    72	
    73	    def get_best_legal_alternative(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> str | None:
    74	        """Given model logits, return the highest-scoring legal move.
    75	
    76	        Args:
    77	            logits: Raw logits of shape (VOCAB_SIZE,).
    78	
    79	        Returns:
    80	            UCI string of the best legal move, or None if no legal moves.
    81	        """
    82	        vocab = get_vocabulary()
    83	        mask = self.get_legal_mask()
    84	
    85	        # Mask out illegal moves and special tokens for this query
    86	        masked = logits.clone()
    87	        masked[~mask] = float("-inf")
    88	
    89	        # Find the best move (excluding NO_MOVE and UNKNOWN)
    90	        best_idx = int(masked[: VOCAB_SIZE - 2].argmax().item())
    91	        if masked[best_idx] == float("-inf"):
    92	            return None
    93	
    94	        return vocab.index_to_uci(best_idx)
    95	
    96	    def is_game_over(self) -> bool:
    97	        """Check if the game has ended."""
    98	        return self.board.is_game_over()
    99	
   100	    def result(self) -> str:
   101	        """Get the game result (e.g. '1-0', '0-1', '1/2-1/2', '*')."""
   102	        if self.board.is_game_over():
   103	            return self.board.result()
   104	        return "*"
   105	
   106	    def to_pgn(
   107	        self,
   108	        white: str = "?",
   109	        black: str = "?",
   110	        event: str = "Argus Reconstruction",
   111	        round_num: str = "?",
   112	    ) -> str:
   113	        """Export the game as a PGN string.
   114	
   115	        Args:
   116	            white: White player name.
   117	            black: Black player name.
   118	            event: Event name.
   119	            round_num: Round number.
   120	
   121	        Returns:
   122	            PGN-formatted string.
   123	        """
   124	        game = chess.pgn.Game()
   125	        game.headers["Event"] = event
   126	        game.headers["White"] = white
   127	        game.headers["Black"] = black
   128	        game.headers["Round"] = round_num
   129	        game.headers["Result"] = self.result()
   130	
   131	        if self._initial_fen != chess.STARTING_FEN:
   132	            game.headers["FEN"] = self._initial_fen
   133	            game.headers["SetUp"] = "1"
   134	
   135	        node: chess.pgn.GameNode = game
   136	        for move in self._move_history:
   137	            node = node.add_variation(move)
   138	
   139	        exporter = chess.pgn.StringExporter(headers=True, variations=False, comments=False)
   140	        return game.accept(exporter)
   141	
   142	    @property
   143	    def move_count(self) -> int:
   144	        """Number of half-moves (plies) played."""
   145	        return len(self._move_history)
   146	
   147	    @property
   148	    def fullmove_number(self) -> int:
   149	        """Current full move number."""
   150	        return self.board.fullmove_number
   151	
   152	    @property
   153	    def turn(self) -> chess.Color:
   154	        """Whose turn it is (chess.WHITE or chess.BLACK)."""
   155	        return self.board.turn
   156	
   157	    def copy(self) -> GameStateMachine:
   158	        """Create a deep copy of this state machine."""
   159	        new = GameStateMachine.__new__(GameStateMachine)
   160	        new.board = self.board.copy()
   161	        new._move_history = list(self._move_history)
   162	        new._initial_fen = self._initial_fen
   163	        return new

1	"""PyTorch Dataset for Argus training and evaluation."""
     2	
     3	from __future__ import annotations
     4	
     5	import logging
     6	from pathlib import Path
     7	from typing import Any
     8	
     9	import torch
    10	from torch.utils.data import Dataset
    11	
    12	from argus.chess.board_state import fen_to_square_targets
    13	
    14	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    15	
    16	
    17	class ArgusDataset(Dataset):  # type: ignore[type-arg]
    18	    """Dataset for Argus chess move detection training.
    19	
    20	    Loads pre-generated clips from disk (produced by synth2d.generate_dataset
    21	    or a real-data pipeline). Each clip is a dict with tensors for frames,
    22	    move targets, detection targets, legal masks, and move masks.
    23	    """
    24	
    25	    def __init__(
    26	        self,
    27	        data_dir: str | Path,
    28	        clip_length: int = 16,
    29	        transform: Any | None = None,
    30	        max_clips: int | None = None,
    31	    ) -> None:
    32	        self.data_dir = Path(data_dir)
    33	        self.clip_length = clip_length
    34	        self.transform = transform
    35	
    36	        self.clip_paths: list[Path] = sorted(self.data_dir.glob("clip_*.pt"))
    37	        if max_clips is not None:
    38	            self.clip_paths = self.clip_paths[:max_clips]
    39	
    40	        if len(self.clip_paths) == 0:
    41	            logger.warning(f"No clip files found in {self.data_dir}")
    42	
    43	    def __len__(self) -> int:
    44	        return len(self.clip_paths)
    45	
    46	    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
    47	        clip_data = torch.load(self.clip_paths[idx], map_location="cpu", weights_only=True)
    48	        frames = clip_data.get("frames")
    49	        if isinstance(frames, torch.Tensor) and frames.shape[-1] == 224 and frames.shape[-2] == 224:
    50	            logger.warning(
    51	                "Degraded 224x224 clip: %s — regenerate for native resolution",
    52	                self.clip_paths[idx].name,
    53	            )
    54	        return _prepare_sample(clip_data, clip_length=self.clip_length, transform=self.transform)
    55	
    56	
    57	class ArgusInMemoryDataset(Dataset):  # type: ignore[type-arg]
    58	    """In-memory dataset from pre-generated clips (e.g., from synth2d)."""
    59	
    60	    def __init__(
    61	        self,
    62	        clips: list[dict[str, Any]],
    63	        clip_length: int = 16,
    64	        transform: Any | None = None,
    65	    ) -> None:
    66	        self.clips = clips
    67	        self.clip_length = clip_length
    68	        self.transform = transform
    69	
    70	    def __len__(self) -> int:
    71	        return len(self.clips)
    72	
    73	    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
    74	        return _prepare_sample(
    75	            self.clips[idx], clip_length=self.clip_length, transform=self.transform
    76	        )
    77	
    78	
    79	def _prepare_sample(
    80	    clip_data: dict[str, Any],
    81	    *,
    82	    clip_length: int,
    83	    transform: Any | None,
    84	) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
    85	    frames = _prepare_frames(clip_data["frames"])
    86	    move_targets = clip_data["move_targets"].to(torch.long)
    87	    detect_targets = clip_data["detect_targets"].to(torch.float32)
    88	    legal_masks = clip_data["legal_masks"].to(torch.bool)
    89	    square_targets = _build_square_targets(clip_data)
    90	    board_corners = _optional_tensor(clip_data.get("board_corners"), dtype=torch.float32)
    91	    move_loss_mask = _optional_tensor(clip_data.get("move_loss_mask"), dtype=torch.bool)
    92	    move_loss_weights = _optional_tensor(clip_data.get("move_loss_weights"), dtype=torch.float32)
    93	
    94	    if transform is not None:
    95	        frames = transform(frames)
    96	
    97	    sequence_length = frames.shape[0]
    98	    if sequence_length < clip_length:
    99	        frames = _pad_tensor(frames, clip_length, dim=0)
   100	        move_targets = _pad_tensor(move_targets, clip_length, dim=0)
   101	        detect_targets = _pad_tensor(detect_targets, clip_length, dim=0)
   102	        legal_masks = _pad_tensor(legal_masks, clip_length, dim=0)
   103	        if square_targets is not None:
   104	            square_targets = _pad_tensor(square_targets, clip_length, dim=0, fill_value=-100)
   105	        if board_corners is not None:
   106	            board_corners = _pad_temporal_tensor_with_last(board_corners, clip_length)
   107	        if move_loss_mask is not None:
   108	            move_loss_mask = _pad_tensor(move_loss_mask, clip_length, dim=0, fill_value=False)
   109	        if move_loss_weights is not None:
   110	            move_loss_weights = _pad_tensor(move_loss_weights, clip_length, dim=0, fill_value=0.0)
   111	    elif sequence_length > clip_length:
   112	        frames = frames[:clip_length]
   113	        move_targets = move_targets[:clip_length]
   114	        detect_targets = detect_targets[:clip_length]
   115	        legal_masks = legal_masks[:clip_length]
   116	        if square_targets is not None:
   117	            square_targets = square_targets[:clip_length]
   118	        if board_corners is not None:
   119	            board_corners = board_corners[:clip_length]
   120	        if move_loss_mask is not None:
   121	            move_loss_mask = move_loss_mask[:clip_length]
   122	        if move_loss_weights is not None:
   123	            move_loss_weights = move_loss_weights[:clip_length]
   124	
   125	    move_mask = _derive_move_mask(detect_targets)
   126	    sample = {
   127	        "frames": frames,
   128	        "move_targets": move_targets,
   129	        "detect_targets": detect_targets,
   130	        "legal_masks": legal_masks,
   131	        "move_mask": move_mask,
   132	    }
   133	    if square_targets is not None:
   134	        sample["square_targets"] = square_targets
   135	    if board_corners is not None:
   136	        sample["board_corners"] = board_corners
   137	    if move_loss_mask is not None:
   138	        sample["move_loss_mask"] = move_loss_mask
   139	    if move_loss_weights is not None:
   140	        sample["move_loss_weights"] = move_loss_weights
   141	    return sample
   142	
   143	
   144	def _prepare_frames(frames: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   145	    """Canonicalize clip frames to float tensors in ``[0, 1]``."""
   146	    if frames.dtype == torch.uint8:
   147	        return frames.to(torch.float32) / 255.0
   148	    return frames.to(torch.float32)
   149	
   150	
   151	def _derive_move_mask(detect_targets: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   152	    """Use detection targets as the source of truth for move-frame masking."""
   153	    return detect_targets > 0.5
   154	
   155	
   156	def _build_square_targets(clip_data: dict[str, Any]) -> torch.Tensor | None:
   157	    fens = clip_data.get("fens")
   158	    if fens is None:
   159	        return None
   160	    board_flipped = clip_data.get("board_flipped", False)
   161	    if isinstance(board_flipped, torch.Tensor):
   162	        board_flipped = bool(board_flipped.item())
   163	    targets = [fen_to_square_targets(fen, board_flipped=bool(board_flipped)) for fen in fens]
   164	    return torch.stack(targets)
   165	
   166	
   167	def _optional_tensor(value: Any, *, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor | None:
   168	    if value is None:
   169	        return None
   170	    tensor: torch.Tensor = value if isinstance(value, torch.Tensor) else torch.tensor(value)
   171	    return tensor.to(dtype=dtype)
   172	
   173	
   174	def _pad_temporal_tensor_with_last(tensor: torch.Tensor, target_len: int) -> torch.Tensor:
   175	    current_len = tensor.shape[0]
   176	    if current_len >= target_len:
   177	        return tensor
   178	    repeat_count = target_len - current_len
   179	    padding = tensor[-1:].expand(repeat_count, *tensor.shape[1:]).clone()
   180	    return torch.cat([tensor, padding], dim=0)
   181	
   182	
   183	def _pad_tensor(
   184	    tensor: torch.Tensor,
   185	    target_len: int,
   186	    dim: int = 0,
   187	    fill_value: int | float = 0,
   188	) -> torch.Tensor:
   189	    """Pad a tensor along a given dimension to target_len."""
   190	    current_len = tensor.shape[dim]
   191	    if current_len >= target_len:
   192	        return tensor
   193	    pad_size = list(tensor.shape)
   194	    pad_size[dim] = target_len - current_len
   195	    padding = torch.full(
   196	        pad_size,
   197	        fill_value=fill_value,
   198	        dtype=tensor.dtype,
   199	        device=tensor.device,
   200	    )
   201	    return torch.cat([tensor, padding], dim=dim)
